key_insight: The paper makes quadratic sum-of-squares optimization faster by replacing costly liftings with a lifting-free regularization that preserves the original conic structure.
engineering_relevance: This can reduce memory and runtime for large constrained regression and learning problems, making SOS-based optimization more practical in computational science workflows.
method: Add a norm penalty to SOS variables to avoid Schur-complement and SOC liftings.
Derive closed-form primal updates and an unconstrained concave dual with Lipschitz gradient.
Solve the dual efficiently with accelerated first-order methods and prove non-asymptotic recovery.
evidence_signal: In constrained regression experiments, the method was reported to be up to 40% faster than SCS and to handle larger instances than MOSEK.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on the new regularization and first-order optimization accuracy, so tuning and convergence behavior may matter on harder or ill-conditioned problems.
